behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the real STT model output (speaker diarization/label quality), not just local wiring. A mocked transcript would hide diarization failures (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly targets STT output quality: "speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels." Rejected Workflow because this behavior is about one model stage, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the production STT API and assert transcript segments retain correct speaker labels for known speakers; verify downstream attribution input receives those labels unchanged.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is deterministic parsing/structuring logic on transcript text, with no required OS/network/model dependency. Failure can be caught at one function/object boundary. Rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on parsing extraction itself, not cross-module state handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser fixture transcripts containing explicit assignments and assert extracted records contain correct assignee and task text fields in the summary-input schema.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM summarization choices (coverage and attribution), which can vary and are not provable with static stubs. Mocked LLM output would trivially pass and miss omission failures. Rejected Workflow because this assertion targets a single summarization decision quality gate, not a multi-agent chain requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Provide real transcript inputs with known per-attendee assignments to the production summarization model; assert final live summary has at least one action item per assigned attendee.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic routing logic after decisions are already identified (per wording: "identified decisions"). Real infra or model output is not required to trigger routing bugs. Rejected Integration because no explicit module-handoff/state propagation failure is required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Given an input object containing pre-identified decisions, assert mapper output places them only under `decisions` schema fields and not in narrative buckets.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: Behavior measures semantic extraction/placement quality of real model output. Deterministic fixtures cannot validate whether the model actually surfaces key decisions versus narrative drift. Rejected Integration due to explicit dependence on "live summary" content quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run production summarization on transcripts with known key decisions and assert those decisions are present in `decisions` section with acceptable recall, not only in narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This behavior requires multiple agentic calls across ordered segments plus merge coherence checks. Failures emerge from step sequencing and cross-step context carryover (MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP), not a single module. Rejected Agentic because behavior text explicitly requires sequential multi-segment processing and merged final coherence.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute recording; execute full segmented pipeline (split -> per-segment transcribe/summarize -> merge) and verify ordering, completeness across segments, and coherent merged output structure.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicit end-to-end orchestration over multiple real AI steps (STT then LLM summarization) and delivery. Real infra and multi-step agentic interactions are both essential; mocks would miss pipeline breakpoints and model-dependent failures. Rejected System because behavior text requires full agent pipeline completion for a real recording, beyond single external-call correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like end-to-end job with a real meeting audio file and assert unattended completion, successful STT and summarization stages, and valid structured output delivery.
